title: AWS Marketplace offer preflight playbook (2026): launch checklist + hero copy templates

h1. Purpose
This playbook is the standard preflight packet for an AWS Marketplace offer (new listing or major revision). It covers:

text:
h1. Purpose
This playbook is the standard preflight packet for an AWS Marketplace offer (new listing or major revision). It covers:
- The required artifacts to unblock Procurement/Legal review
- Recommended SKU structure and metering defaults
- Approved hero copy templates (with compliance-safe language)
- A one-page co-sell blurb template for AWS Partner Ops

This page was created after repeated partner asks for a “short checklist + 2–3 hero copy options” to speed internal alignment.

h1. When to use
Use this playbook when:
- A partner requests an AWS Marketplace listing (public listing, private offer, or both)
- A deal is targeting AWS co-sell registration
- Procurement requests a “preflight checklist” before engaging Legal/Security

h1. Owners and turnaround expectations
- DRI (Partners/Marketplace): Partnerships Director (default)
- Messaging: Product Marketing
- Packaging/Meterin...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook covers triage and mitigation for elevated latency and/or 5xx error rate on the **Suggestions API** used by the Redwood Console “Optimization suggestions” beta.

Primary goals:
- Restore availability and latency SLOs quickly.
- Prevent cross-tenant or broad Console impact (UI timeouts, repeated retries).
- Preserve correctness guarantees (RBAC + org/project scoping) while degraded.

Non-goals:
- Deep root cause analysis (capture data for follow-up; open an incident or ticket as needed).

---

## Service / Endpoint Overview
**User-facing surface:** Redwood Console → Optimize → Suggestions

**Backend service:** `optimize-suggestions-service`

**Primary endpoint (internal naming):**
- `GET /optimize/suggestions` (list; paginated, sortable)
- `GET /optimize/suggestions/{suggestion_id}` (details)

**Common query parameters:**
- `org_id`, `project_id`
- `route_id` (op...

- **Fast** (single action from Console or API)

text:
## Purpose
This runbook describes how to execute a **one-click rollback** of an Optimization Config version (batching, caching, and quantization profiles) for Hosted and Dedicated deployments, and how to validate recovery.

Rollback is intended to be:
- **Fast** (single action from Console or API)
- **Safe** (idempotent + race-condition guarded)
- **Auditable** (structured audit events)
- **Scoped** (tenant/model/region aware)

## Audience
- SRE on-call
- Eng Platform (Control Plane) on-call
- Runtime on-call
- Console on-call

## Definitions
- **Optimization Config**: Serving-time knobs (batching heuristics, prefix/KV cache toggles, quantization profile selection) applied by the runtime.
- **Config Version**: Immutable, versioned object (e.g., `ocv_2026_02_24_0017`).
- **Active Version**: The version currently pinned for a given scope (tenant/model/region).
- **Apply / Rollout**: Sta...

text:
## Purpose
This page explains how to enable the **Optimization Suggestions (batching/caching) beta** for a specific customer org using feature flags.

This is an internal-only operational procedure used by Product/CS/Solutions and on-call engineering during beta rollout.

---

## What gets enabled
The beta is gated by **two controls**:

1) **Console UI flag** (shows the Suggestions entry point, list view, and details drawer)
- Flag key: `console.optimize_suggestions_beta`

2) **Backend API allowlist flag** (permits `/optimize/suggestions` reads for the org)
- Flag key: `optimize.suggestions_api_enabled`

Both flags must be enabled for the org to have a functional experience.

Why two flags?
- The API flag prevents accidental exposure via direct API calls.
- The UI flag allows gradual UX rollout and easy UI-only rollback without fully disabling the service.

---

## Ownership and appro...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook covers how to enable Shadow Traffic safely, how to monitor and control overhead, and how to disable/rollback quickly if the shadow path (routing, telemetry, scoring) degrades.

Shadow Traffic duplicates a sampled subset of production requests to one or more shadow targets (candidate variants) while returning the control response to the user. It must be **fail-open**: any shadow failures must not impact the user path.

**Primary owners**
- Data plane / routing: Mina Haddad / Ravi Menon / Vikram Singh
- Control plane policy: Ethan Park / Benji Okafor / Jordan Lee
- Telemetry pipelines: Logan Wright / Nadia Rahman / Caleb Johnson
- Scoring workers / eval-harness integration: Jonas Weber / Karthik Iyer / Olivia Grant
- SRE / alerting: Sean Gallagher / Allison Grant
- Security controls: Marcus Lin / Kevin Osei

---

title: Runbook: Optimize Quality Gate Failures (triage, mitigation, rollback, escalation)
preview:
## Purpose
This runbook covers operational response when **Optimize quality gates** fail, flap, or time out. It is intended for SRE oncall and incident commanders during beta/limited production rollout.

Quality gates are mandatory evaluation checks required before applying Optimize recommendations (batching, prefix...
text:
## Purpose
This runbook covers operational response when **Optimize quality gates** fail, flap, or time out. It is intended for SRE oncall and incident commanders during beta/limited production rollout.

Quality gates are mandatory evaluation checks required before applying Optimize recommendations (batching, prefix/KV caching, quantization profile changes) to production traffic.

This runbook focuses on:
- Triage steps (what failed, where, why)
- Distinguishing **real quality regressions** vs **eval flakiness / infrastructure issues**
- Safe mitigations (pause/rollback/disable) and when to use overrides
- Escalation paths and required artifacts

## When to use this runbook
Use this runbook when any of the following occurs:
- A production or staging rollout is **blocked** due to `quality_gate_failed`.
- Gate run pass rate drops sharply (potential system issue or a regression).
- Gate ...

title: SLO definitions for rollout safety (auto-pause/auto-rollback signals)
preview:
## Purpose
This document defines which Service Level Objectives (SLOs) are eligible to act as *safety signals* for Redwood rollouts (canary, A/B, staged model deployments) and provides recommended thresholds by customer tier.

These SLOs are consumed by the rollout controller’s SLO evaluator to decide whether to **p...
text:
## Purpose
This document defines which Service Level Objectives (SLOs) are eligible to act as *safety signals* for Redwood rollouts (canary, A/B, staged model deployments) and provides recommended thresholds by customer tier.

These SLOs are consumed by the rollout controller’s SLO evaluator to decide whether to **pause** a rollout or **rollback** traffic to the last known-good baseline.

## Scope
Applies to:
- Hosted API routes (multi-tenant)
- Dedicated capacity routes
- Private (VPC/on-prem) deployments where Redwood operates/observes the serving plane

Out of scope for initial release:
- Quality/regression eval signals (handled by eval/guardrails)
- Multi-region failover (beyond rollback/pause)

## Terminology
- **Rollout scope**: the specific tuple the evaluator queries against: *(service/route, model version, region, customer tier)*.
- **Baseline**: currently promoted/last-known...

title: ADR: Audit Event Delivery Guarantees (Ingestion, Buffering, and Outage Behavior)
preview:
## Status
Published (effective for schema v1 rollout).

## Summary
This ADR defines Redwood Inference’s delivery semantics for audit events and how producers must behave during audit pipeline degradation or outages.

We choose **at-least-once delivery from the audit ingestion service onward** (durable queue → storag...
text:
## Status
Published (effective for schema v1 rollout).

## Summary
This ADR defines Redwood Inference’s delivery semantics for audit events and how producers must behave during audit pipeline degradation or outages.

We choose **at-least-once delivery from the audit ingestion service onward** (durable queue → storage/export), and **best-effort delivery from producers to ingestion** with **bounded buffering and retry**. This prioritizes platform availability (admin actions must not routinely fail) while still providing strong audit reliability, clear signals when delivery is degraded, and a consistent customer guidance story (including how to de-duplicate in SIEMs).

## Context
Redwood is standardizing audit logs across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private. Customers (especially regulated/enterprise) require:
- Consistent auditability of sensitive actions.
- Reasonable delivery latency to th...

text:
## Purpose
This page is the reference for how Support SLAs (first response + update cadence) should map to Engineering SLOs (availability/latency) and how paging thresholds should relate to *customer impact*.

This is **not** a contractual SLA document. For contract language and tier-specific coverage windows, refer to: **Customer Success & Support → SLA policy by customer tier**.

**Goals**
- Prevent mismatches where Support promises a cadence that Engineering on-call cannot meet.
- Ensure paging triggers correlate to real customer impact (not only internal “red” alerts).
- Standardize language between Support (Jira priorities P0–P4) and Engineering incident severity (Sev0–Sev3).

---

## Definitions (shared language)
**Support SLA (operational):** Response/update targets used by Support/CS for incident comms and ticket handling. These are constrained by coverage windows and the curr...

title: Shadow Traffic: SLOs, Error Budgets, and Guardrails (v1)

This page defines production SLOs, error budgets, and safety guardrails for the Shadow Traffic capability (mirroring sampled production requests to shadow variants and scoring outputs asynchronously). The primary reliability requirement is **fail-open** behavior: user traffic must not be degraded by shado...
text:
## Summary
This page defines production SLOs, error budgets, and safety guardrails for the Shadow Traffic capability (mirroring sampled production requests to shadow variants and scoring outputs asynchronously). The primary reliability requirement is **fail-open** behavior: user traffic must not be degraded by shadowing failures.

This document covers:
- Overhead budgets for the user-serving (control) path
- Acceptable error rates/latency for the shadow path and scoring pipeline
- Alert thresholds (page vs ticket) and required dashboards
- Guardrails (circuit breakers, backpressure, cost caps)

Applies to: Hosted API, Dedicated, and Private deployments (with local equivalents for Private).

**Owners**
- SRE owner: Sean Gallagher
- Observability/alerting: Allison Grant
- Telemetry pipeline: Logan Wright / Nadia Rahman
- Smart routing integration: Mina Haddad / Ravi Menon
- Scoring/Eval...

title: ADR-0127: SLO breach evaluation strategy for rollout auto-actions (burn-rate, sample gating, debounce/holdoff)
preview:
## Status
Published (effective for Auto-rollback on SLO breach MVP)

## Context
Redwood’s rollout controller (canary, staged rollouts, and A/B) is being integrated with latency and error-rate SLO monitors to automatically **pause** or **rollback** a rollout when an SLO breach occurs.

A naïve “threshold exceeded -> ...
text:
## Status
Published (effective for Auto-rollback on SLO breach MVP)

## Context
Redwood’s rollout controller (canary, staged rollouts, and A/B) is being integrated with latency and error-rate SLO monitors to automatically **pause** or **rollback** a rollout when an SLO breach occurs.

A naïve “threshold exceeded -> rollback immediately” implementation causes **oscillation** and **false positives** due to:
- Natural tail latency variability during small-traffic canary steps.
- Metric ingestion lag and partial telemetry outages.
- Transient bursts (GC pauses, noisy neighbors, autoscaler step changes).
- SLO monitor windowing effects (brief spikes shouldn’t always cause action).

We need a breach evaluation strategy that is:
- Sensitive to real regressions (fast detection).
- Robust against noise (avoids flapping/paging storms).
- Consistent across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployme...

title: Reliability Plan: Safe Apply + Rollback for Optimization Configs
preview:
## Summary
This document defines the reliability targets, observability requirements, alerting strategy, and operational KPIs for **Safe Apply + Rollback** of versioned **Optimization Configs** (batching, caching, and quantization profiles) across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments.

The intent is to make op...
text:
## Summary
This document defines the reliability targets, observability requirements, alerting strategy, and operational KPIs for **Safe Apply + Rollback** of versioned **Optimization Configs** (batching, caching, and quantization profiles) across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments.

The intent is to make optimization/config changes:
- **Predictable:** gated by health/quality signals before broad rollout.
- **Reversible:** one-click rollback with clear semantics and bounded time-to-mitigate.
- **Auditable:** end-to-end traceability (who/what/when/where) and correlation to SLO impact.

Owner: **SRE (Eng-SRE)** in partnership with **Platform Control Plane** and **Serving Runtime**.

---

## Goals
1. Define **SLOs** for config-change operations (validate/apply/promote/rollback) and the surrounding rollout controller.
2. Specify **gating signal requirements** (latency, errors, sat...

title: Hosted API tail-latency guardrails for KV cache compaction/eviction rollout
preview:
## Purpose
This page defines SRE-facing guardrails to protect Hosted API tail latency (p95/p99) during the staged rollout of KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction in the serving runtime.

The rollout introduces new runtime behaviors (budgeted compaction work and eviction under pressure) that can improve long-tai...
text:
## Purpose
This page defines SRE-facing guardrails to protect Hosted API tail latency (p95/p99) during the staged rollout of KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction in the serving runtime.

The rollout introduces new runtime behaviors (budgeted compaction work and eviction under pressure) that can improve long-tail performance under bursty mixed-length decode workloads, but can also regress latency if:
- compaction work is performed on the critical path more than intended,
- eviction selection becomes too aggressive for certain traffic mixes,
- allocator state oscillates (fragmentation ↔ compaction ↔ eviction) and increases CPU/GPU overhead,
- cancellation/streaming edge cases increase lock contention.

This page standardizes:
- **SLO targets** and what counts as a rollback-worthy regression
- **Alert thresholds** and paging policy during the canary
- **Automated rollback criteria**...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook defines how we size and operate capacity for the **sales demo environment** (the “demo tenant”) and how we configure **quotas and rate limits** so multiple AEs/SEs can run demos in parallel without impacting one another.

This page is written for:
- **SEs** running live demos who hit 429s / latency spikes
- **Eng Infra / SRE** responding to demo-capacity incidents
- **Eng Platform** owners of quota + control-plane config

Non-goals:
- Deep “how to reset the tenant” steps (see *Demo Tenant Reset Runbook*)
- General product pricing limits (this is for demo-only capacity)

---

## TL;DR (for the field)
If a live demo starts failing due to capacity:
1) **Stop synthetic traffic** for your project (reduces load immediately).
2) **Switch to the backup route/region** (pinned compatible model variant).
3) **Request a quota bump** using the “Demo Hotfix” procedure below....

text:
## Purpose
This page defines service-level objectives (SLOs) and internal service-level commitments for Redwood’s **Sales Demo Environment** ("demo tenant"). The demo environment must be reliable enough for AE/SE live demos, while staying safe (synthetic data only) and cheap to operate.

**Important:** These targets are for *internal reliability management*. They are not customer-facing SLAs and should not be quoted as contractual commitments.

## Scope
In scope (what we measure and page on):
- **Demo Inference API** (hosted API entrypoint + routing + model pinning/fallback behavior for demo routes)
- **Streaming** behavior (time-to-first-token and disconnect rate)
- **Console Demo Views** (dashboards, rollout view, tracing/error analysis pages used in the talk track)
- **Telemetry ingestion for demo** (synthetic traffic → events → dashboards)
- **Demo reset + healthcheck** (SE-facing...

title: Runbook: Offline bundle signature/manifest verification failures (Redwood Private)
preview:
## Purpose
This runbook is used by SRE/Support/PS to diagnose and resolve **offline bundle verification failures** for Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem), especially in **air-gapped** environments.

Verification covers:
- Bundle manifest integrity (hashes)
- Manifest signature validity (trusted key, signature matches man...
text:
## Purpose
This runbook is used by SRE/Support/PS to diagnose and resolve **offline bundle verification failures** for Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem), especially in **air-gapped** environments.

Verification covers:
- Bundle manifest integrity (hashes)
- Manifest signature validity (trusted key, signature matches manifest)
- Certificate validity window checks (not-yet-valid / expired)
- Downgrade/replay protection (signed version + local install state)

**Safety principle:** installer verification is designed to **fail closed**. Do not bypass verification except via an approved break-glass process.

---

## Symptoms (what you will see)
Typical user-facing errors during `preflight` / `install` include:
- **HASH_MISMATCH**: One or more artifacts do not match the manifest digest.
- **MANIFEST_PARSE_ERROR**: Manifest cannot be parsed or canonicalization mismatch.
- **SIGNATURE_INVALID**: S...

## Purpose
This document defines reliability targets (SLOs), monitoring, and escalation paths for the **offline bundle signing and publication pipeline** used by Redwood Private (VPC + on‑prem / air‑gapped). The objective is to keep customer deployments unblocked by signing-related issues while maintaining strong fail-closed security properties.

## Scope
In scope:
- Deterministic build + manifest generation for Redwood Private offline bundles.
- Cryptographic signing of the bundle manifest (and/or signature envelope) using Redwood-controlled keys.
- Publishing of signed bundle artifacts to approved distribution endpoints (internal artifact store + customer delivery mechanism).
- Verification gating required for cutting a Private release.

title: ADR: Safe Apply Gating Signals for Optimization Config Rollouts
preview:
## Status
Published (effective for Hosted + Dedicated rollouts). Private deployments must implement equivalent metrics and can use the same thresholds as defaults.

## Context
Redwood Optimize allows high-impact runtime configuration changes (batching heuristics, caching toggles/knobs, and quantization profiles). Hi...
text:
## Status
Published (effective for Hosted + Dedicated rollouts). Private deployments must implement equivalent metrics and can use the same thresholds as defaults.

## Context
Redwood Optimize allows high-impact runtime configuration changes (batching heuristics, caching toggles/knobs, and quantization profiles). Historically, config changes have caused:
- Latency regressions (p95/p99 spikes) that were not detected during early rollout steps.
- Elevated 5xx/timeout rates due to semantic misconfiguration or resource exhaustion.
- "Silent" quality regressions with quantization profiles that only surfaced in downstream customer behavior.
- Operational ambiguity during incidents (unclear whether to pause, continue, or rollback) due to inconsistent metrics and missing baselines.

This ADR defines **the gating signals** used by the Safe Apply controller to determine whether a staged rollout...

## Purpose
This runbook defines the supported, low-risk procedure for upgrading the **Redwood Private VPC Terraform modules (alpha)** in customer or Redwood-managed AWS accounts, and how to rollback safely if an upgrade introduces networking regressions.

Scope:
- Applies to the `redwood-terraform` module suite used for **AWS VPC-based private deployments** (EKS assumed).
- Focuses on networking + foundational infra modules (VPC/subnets/routing/NAT, endpoints/DNS, security groups, flow logs/log sinks, IAM scaffolding).
- Applies to both **design partner pilots** and internal dogfood environments.

Non-goals:
- Full application/cluster rollback (handled by `redwood-private-installer` + Helm rollouts).
- Major topology migrations (e.g., CIDR changes, multi-VPC redesign). These require an engagement plan and change window.

- Infra (modules + C...

title: Postmortem: Latency regression after Optimization Config rollout (rollback required)
preview:
## Summary
On 2025-08-14, a production rollout of an Optimization Config change (batching heuristics + cache toggle for select routes) caused elevated p95/p99 latency for Hosted inference traffic in us-east-1 and eu-west-1. The change was intended to reduce per-token cost via more aggressive continuous batching. Wit...
text:
## Summary
On 2025-08-14, a production rollout of an Optimization Config change (batching heuristics + cache toggle for select routes) caused elevated p95/p99 latency for Hosted inference traffic in us-east-1 and eu-west-1. The change was intended to reduce per-token cost via more aggressive continuous batching. Within ~15 minutes, customer-facing latency breached SLOs and a subset of tenants experienced timeouts.

Mitigation required a manual rollback by re-applying a prior optimization config snapshot, coordinated via on-call and runtime operators. Because the config was not versioned and the apply mechanism was not transactional/idempotent, rollback was slower than desired and required multiple retries to converge worker state.

This incident is the primary driver for the “Safe apply + rollback mechanism” project: introduce immutable config versions, staged rollout with gates, one-...

title: Dedicated Burst Capacity: SLOs, Error Budgets, and Alerting Policy
preview:
## Purpose
This page defines Service Level Objectives (SLOs), error budgets, and alerting policy for **Dedicated Burst Capacity**.

The guiding principle is: **burst must never regress baseline Dedicated reliability for non-bursting tenants**. Bursting is a paid add-on and may be denied; denial is not, by itself, a ...
text:
## Purpose
This page defines Service Level Objectives (SLOs), error budgets, and alerting policy for **Dedicated Burst Capacity**.

The guiding principle is: **burst must never regress baseline Dedicated reliability for non-bursting tenants**. Bursting is a paid add-on and may be denied; denial is not, by itself, a breach as long as denial behavior is consistent with documented limits and the platform remains reliable.

This document is normative for:
- What we protect (non-burst tenants first)
- What we measure (SLIs and metric sources)
- When we page (and when we ticket)
- Rollout gates for beta/GA by region and hardware class

## Scope
### In scope
- Dedicated requests served from reserved capacity (baseline)
- Dedicated requests served using burst pools (burst path)
- Admission controller behavior and routing integration
- Fleet safety caps and global circuit breaker behavior

###...

text:
## Purpose
This page defines the reliability targets and escalation mechanics for the **Runtime LTS channel** across **Hosted API**, **Dedicated**, and **Private** deployments.

It answers:
- What SLOs we commit to for LTS traffic (and what we measure).
- What happens when we are burning error budget (who gets paged, who decides, and what the required actions are).
- When an LTS patch is **Mandatory** vs **Recommended/Optional**, and how we communicate that to Support/CS/Sales.

## Scope
**In scope**
- Runtime LTS branches (e.g., `runtime-1.18-lts`) and all published patch releases (e.g., `1.18.1-lts`).
- LTS traffic in Hosted and Dedicated.
- LTS artifacts installed via Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem) when customer is pinned to an LTS patch.

**Out of scope**
- Mainline runtime SLOs and escalation (covered elsewhere).
- Feature delivery expectations on LTS (LTS prioritizes stability; n...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook covers day-2 operations for Redwood’s retention purge jobs (TTL enforcement) across supported data stores. It explains how to:
- Monitor purge health and retention “TTL lag”
- Safely re-run purge jobs (idempotent execution)
- Handle purge backlog without causing database pressure or telemetry regressions
- Verify deletions and produce internal evidence for audits/customer requests

This is the on-call reference for incidents like “TTL enforcement delayed in a region” or “purge backlog after maintenance”.

## Scope (what this runbook covers)
In-scope purge jobs (v1):
- Request/response logs store (a.k.a. request logs)
- Audit logs store
- Console traces (where enabled) and related metadata indexes
- Metrics retention (downsampling/expiration where applicable)

Out of scope (handled by separate runbooks or vendor lifecycle policies):
- Customer-managed object sto...

title: Dedicated rollout gates: SLO alignment and error-budget-based promotion policy

text:
## Purpose
This page defines the **default rollout gate thresholds** for **Dedicated staged rollouts** and maps them explicitly to Dedicated **availability, latency, and capacity (saturation/headroom)** SLOs.

It also documents the **error-budget-based promotion policy** used by SRE and the rollout policy engine to decide whether a rollout stage can be promoted automatically.

This doc is intended for:
- SRE on-call and SRE stakeholders supporting Dedicated
- Platform + Routing engineers implementing gate evaluation and promotion semantics
- Product/Console teams who surface gate evidence and promotion constraints

## Scope
In-scope gates (v1):
- Request success/error rates (5xx, 429, dropped requests)
- Latency (p95/p99)
- Saturation/headroom (GPU utilization, queue depth, token throughput headroom)
- GPU health (node taints/ECC/throttling summarized at pool partition level)
- OOM / ...

title: ADR-027: Dedicated Throughput SLO Metric Definition (Canonical Metric + Attribution Model)
preview:
## Status
Accepted (ADR-027)

## Decision summary
We will use **Effective Executed Tokens per Second (tps_executed_effective)** as the canonical throughput metric for Dedicated SLO evaluation, and we will attribute/measure the SLO **per Dedicated deployment (deployment_id)** rather than per route.

We will continue ...
text:
## Status
Accepted (ADR-027)

## Decision summary
We will use **Effective Executed Tokens per Second (tps_executed_effective)** as the canonical throughput metric for Dedicated SLO evaluation, and we will attribute/measure the SLO **per Dedicated deployment (deployment_id)** rather than per route.

We will continue to emit **per-route** and **customer-delivered** throughput metrics for debugging, customer reporting, and product analytics, but they are **not** the SLO source of truth.

---

## Context
Redwood Dedicated is formalizing a throughput guarantee. We need a canonical metric definition that is:
- **Stable** across rollouts (canaries/A-B), routing changes, and model upgrades.
- **Hard to game** (e.g., by priming caches or manipulating streaming chunking).
- **Comparable** across regions/hardware shapes while acknowledging model-dependent variance.
- **Actionable** for SRE/Infra...

text:
h1. Purpose
This runbook covers production incidents where *Hosted API data residency enforcement* incorrectly blocks legitimate traffic ("false positives"). It provides:

* Triage and verification steps
* Immediate mitigation options (including feature flag overrides) with safety constraints
* Customer communication guidance
* Rollback procedure and post-incident follow-ups

This runbook applies to the residency enforcement path across:

* API Gateway (request validation + error contract)
* Smart Routing (region selection + fallback restrictions)
* Residency Policy Service (control plane policy evaluation)
* Observability/audit logging related to enforcement decisions

h1. Symptoms & Detection
h2. Common symptoms
* Sudden spike in blocked requests for one or more regions, orgs, or projects.
* Customers report requests failing after enabling region pinning.
* Support reports inconsist...

title: Postmortem: 2025-02-17 Sev1 — Tensor-parallel decode hang causing Dedicated timeouts
preview:
## Executive summary
On 2025-02-17, a Dedicated customer experienced elevated request timeouts and streaming stalls due to a tensor-parallel (TP) decode hang in the serving runtime on A100-80GB multi-GPU replicas.

A subset of TP replicas entered a state where one or more ranks stopped making forward progress in the...
text:
## Executive summary
On 2025-02-17, a Dedicated customer experienced elevated request timeouts and streaming stalls due to a tensor-parallel (TP) decode hang in the serving runtime on A100-80GB multi-GPU replicas.

A subset of TP replicas entered a state where one or more ranks stopped making forward progress in the decode loop while other ranks continued to enqueue work, resulting in blocked NCCL collectives and wedged decode steps. This manifested as requests that never completed (hangs), triggering downstream timeouts and cascading retries.

Immediate mitigations (replica drain/recycle and temporary concurrency caps) restored service while we collected logs and narrowed the most likely failure mode. This incident materially accelerated the “Multi-GPU (TP) stability fixes” program (ENG-4821).

## Customer impact
**Primary impacted customer:** Acme AI (Dedicated, A100-80GB, TP=4 per ...
links: Linear: ENG-4821 Multi-GPU (TP) stability fixes initiative
Linear: ENG-4823 Add TP heartbeat metrics and stall detectors
Linear: ENG-4824 Scheduler fix — collective ordering starvation

Linear: ENG-4826 TP watchdog timeout and safe abort

title: How-to: Configure region pinning in staging (Hosted data residency enforcement)

text:
## Overview
This page documents the staging-only procedure to:
- Enable Hosted data residency enforcement feature flags.
- Create or update a test org/project with a residency policy (allowed regions + primary region).
- Generate test API keys (including optional API-key-level overrides).
- Run verification scenarios to confirm:
 - Requests route only within the pinned region set.
 - Cross-region fallback is blocked by default.
 - Error responses are deterministic and match the public error contract.
 - Decision context is present in logs/metrics/audit events.

This is intended for engineers, SREs, and on-call validating rollouts before allowlisting customers.

---

## Terminology (staging)
- **Allowed regions**: The set of regions the request may be processed in (e.g., `eu-west-1`, `eu-central-1`).
- **Primary region**: Preferred region within the allowed set.
- **Cross-region fallba...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook describes the periodic smoke test SRE performs to ensure Redwood Private backup artifacts are usable and that a restore into a staging environment is repeatable.

This runbook is used for:
- Weekly (or per-customer requirement) integrity checks of the most recent control-plane backup.
- Scheduled restore drills into a dedicated staging cluster.
- Alerting and paging guidance when checks/drills fail.

## Scope
In scope (smoke test):
- Validate the **latest control-plane backup** exists, can be decrypted, and passes integrity checks.
- Perform a **restore drill into staging** using the supported installer workflow.
- Run post-restore validation (health, API checks, basic tenancy/config verification).
- Record drill duration and outcomes; trigger paging/escalation per policy.

Out of scope:
- Full region-loss DR execution (see DR plans).
- Cluster-level recovery (...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook describes how to restore the **Redwood Private control plane** from a previously created backup artifact.

It covers:
- Restore prerequisites (cluster, tooling, access, keys)
- Restore modes (in-place vs. new cluster)
- Bootstrap order and dependency sequencing
- Validation (functional + data integrity)
- Abort/rollback criteria
- How to confirm **tenant configuration** and **routing policies** are restored correctly

This runbook is intended for:
- Redwood SRE / Private Deployments teams running DR drills or assisting customers
- Customer platform teams operating a supported Redwood Private deployment

---

## Scope and non-goals
### In scope (this runbook)
- Restoring Redwood control-plane state captured by the Redwood backup toolkit (configuration database, control-plane object/state, release metadata, policy/config state)
- Restoring Kubernetes objects that...

The metric is executive-visible (Finance + Product + Engineering) and is used for quarter-to-date reporting. The pipeline must be reliable enough for weekly...
text:
## Purpose
This page defines the **SLOs, SLIs, alerting, and escalation paths** for the Unit Economics pipeline powering the Internal Unit Economics Dashboard.

The metric is executive-visible (Finance + Product + Engineering) and is used for quarter-to-date reporting. The pipeline must be reliable enough for weekly exec readouts while still allowing iteration on attribution logic.

## Scope
**In scope:**
- Token metering inputs (with emphasis on **output tokens**, including streaming paths)
- Hourly aggregation job + warehouse tables/views used by the dashboard
- Dimension coverage: model/version, region, customer tier, quantization_profile, streaming flag
- Daily reconciliation view vs billing and Finance rate card inputs

**Out of scope:**
- Console/UI availability SLOs
- External customer-facing reporting

## Service boundary and owners
**Logical system:** Unit Economics Pipeline
...
